titleStmt.title: Letter from Emma Duffin to her mother Maria Duffin, 27 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Emma Duffin

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
This letter is part of a rich correspondence between various members of the Duffin family, a large prosperous family living in Strandtown, Belfast. Several family members served in the war, including Major Terence Duffin, who was a staff officer with 107 Brigade, and later with Royal Irish Rifles; Major Charles G Duffin MC, Royal Field Artillery; and their sisters Emma, Celia and Dorothy who served as Voluntary Aid Detachments (VADS) in Egypt and France, and with the YMCA.
This letter was sent by Emma (1883 - 1979) to her mother Maria (1854 – 1954). Emma was born in Belfast and educated at Cheltenham Ladies College. She served initially in Egypt before being transferred to northern France just before the Battle of the Somme, where she remained to the Armistice in 1918.
In this letter Emma tells her mother of her plans to meet with her sisters Dorothy, Celia and Olive. Before signing off, Emma expresses her sorrow at the death of her aunt Margaret.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

correspAction.placeName: Strand Palace Hotel, Strand, London W.C., England

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
STRAND PALACE HOTEL,
STRAND,
LONDON,W.C. March 27/16 My dearest Mamma I got your wire, I suppose
you were astonished to get mine, but
we only were given a few hours notice &
I saw no advantage in cabling as it would
only have made you anxious, especially
as we were not able to give the name of
the ship. I am arriving home by
Larne & Stranraer tomorrow, Tuesday. I wired Olive who came to meet me
& stayed last night. Dorothy is staying
with me tonight & Celia is trying to get
a half day, today as she was only able
to rush up yesterday for an hour. I was terribly shocked to hear of dear
Aunt Margaret's death. I cannot realise
it yet at all! She was the most
patient unselfish person I ever met and
always so good and sweet to me. I
dread meeting poor Marjorie. She will
feel so terribly alone. I won't write more now dearest, it will
be lovely to see you all again Best love to Papa Ever your own Emma
